sourceMovie.title: The Theory of Everything
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: The Theory of Everything works as a drama movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The drama and romance blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: He's Just Not That Into You
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Theory of Everything clicked for you, He's Just Not That Into You (2009) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama and romance, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: The Time Traveler's Wife
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Theory of Everything clicked for you, The Time Traveler's Wife (2009) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama and romance, with emotional whiplash that feels honest. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after The Theory of Everything?
faq.answer: Try He's Just Not That Into You, The Time Traveler's Wife, Never Let Me Go—each shares DNA with The Theory of Everything while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is The Theory of Everything?
faq.answer: The Theory of Everything (2014) is best described as Drama, Romance. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is The Theory of Everything worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.8/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this drama lane.
